engagement.name: BlackOps Stage 01 — Intelligence on Ourselves
engagement.target_artefact: Intelligence Report for NY May 2026 pitch

framework.name: Alpha Suite — Discovery v2
framework.notes: Substrate is filesystem-as-source-of-truth; atoms live in atoms/; heterogeneous sources live in corpus/; URIs below can resolve to any format and any backend — resolver is viewer-side. Supported schemes include file://, https://, gdrive://, dropbox://, s3://, sharepoint://, miro://, figjam://, figma://, loom://, notion://, youtube://. Non-markdown is first-class: images, video, audio, PDFs, spreadsheets, board exports, and external-tool canvases all live in corpus/ subdirectories and are registered here.

atoms.title: Core Problem — Founder execution gap and fund-level Adoption Alpha loss
Antler — Day-zero talent-first venture studio; closest structural comparable to BlackOps
a16z — Full-service VC platform; proves execution-layer gap at the top of the market
Scattergun-Slither — founder thinks they know, spreads thin, drifts to slop
Compelling Differentiation — BlackOps' core positioning move
atoms.open_probe: Definition of 'compelling' — tell vs. bundle. Load-bearing; interview paused here for substrate setup.

hypotheses.claim: Funds who have engaged with Antler will have a more sceptical 'another-one-of-those' reaction — segment NY pitch list accordingly
Mid-tier VCs (Fund II/III, $100M–$500M AUM) are the primary BlackOps customer — cannot build a16z-scale platform but feel the gap acutely

pitch-deck.purpose: The April 2026 deck and supporting narrative. Canonical text lives in ../context-blackops.md.

brand-assets.purpose: Holding page source, preview renders. Deployed to blackops.design.

competitors.purpose: Teardowns of fund-embedded studios and adjacent models (Antler, EF, a16z, Atomic, Pioneer Square Labs, Betaworks, High Alpha).
competitors.status: partial — 2 of ~7 teardowns drafted (Antler, a16z); remaining: EF, Atomic, Pioneer Square Labs, Betaworks, High Alpha; all single-model, triangulation upgrade pending

interviews.purpose: VC partner interviews + operator/founder Discovery sessions. Load-bearing for BO-PROBLEM-001 validation; operator session feeds BO-PERSONA-OPERATOR-001, BO-PROBLEM-002, BO-POSITION-001.
interviews.status: 1 operator Discovery in progress; 0 VC partner interviews — VC side remains highest priority for Section 7, must be booked before NY

market.purpose: TAM/SAM/SOM for fund-embedded studio category; Startup Genome, McKinsey Design Index, CB Insights data.

regulatory.purpose: Fund structuring, SPV rules, equity-in-round legal constraints. Gemini long-context candidate.

why-now.purpose: Post-2023 VC power-law compression, AI-native tooling surge, fund platform fatigue.

context.purpose: Supporting thinking that informs atoms but isn't itself a deliverable source.

gate.name: Intelligence-to-Architecture Gate
questions: Does the pitch's primary message deliver on the core pain identified here?
Does the monetization model (equity-in-round) match what VCs actually sign up for?
Does the positioning honour what already works (track record, Talos intros) without leaning on it as the whole story?
Have we checked our own bias — building what Intelligence says to build, or what we find interesting to build?
